2604.27264.title: Self-Evolving Software Agents
2604.27264.paper_summary: Autonomous agents can adapt their behaviour to changing environments, but remain bound to requirements, goals, and capabilities fixed at design time, preventing genuine software evolution. This paper introduces self-evolving software agents, combining BDI reasoning with LLMs to enable autonomous evolution of goals, reasoning, and executable code. We propose a BDI-LLM architecture in which an automated evolution module operates alongside the agent's reasoning loop, eliciting new requirements from experience and synthesizing corresponding design and code updates. A prototype evaluated in a dynamic multi-agent environment shows that agents can autonomously discover new goals and generate executable behaviours from minimal prior knowledge. The results indicate both the feasibility and current limits of LLM-driven evolution, particularly in terms of behavioural inheritance and stability.
2604.27264.paper_summary_zh: 自主代理可以根據變化的環境調整其行為，但仍然受到設計時固定的需求、目標和能力的限制，這阻礙了真正的軟體演化。本文介紹了自我演化的軟體代理，結合BDI推理和LLM，使目標、推理和可執行代碼的自主演化成為可能。我們提出了一種BDI-LLM架構，其中自動演化模塊與代理的推理循環並行運作，從經驗中引出新需求並合成相應的設計和代碼更新。在一個動態多代理環境中評估的原型顯示，代理可以自主發現新目標並從最少的先驗知識中生成可執行行為。結果表明了LLM驅動演化的可行性及當前的限制，特別是在行為繼承和穩定性方面。
2604.27264.author: Marco Robol et.al.
